When Earth's air becomes unbreathable, a message from the future sends a man on a dangerous mission to an unknown world to save the human race.

programme.title: Airborne
programme.desc: A plane takes off during a snowstorm and soon reports both pilots are dead. As the passengers slowly die off, they wish they had never left the ground.

programme.title: All Hallows' Eve 2
programme.desc: A woman finds a VHS tape that shows a series of gruesome tales.

programme.title: ATM
programme.desc: A murderous assailant traps three people (Alice Eve, Josh Peck, Brian Geraghty) in a small kiosk containing an automatic teller machine.

programme.title: Backcountry
programme.desc: A couple (Missy Peregrym, Jeff Roop) on a deep-wilderness hike become hopelessly lost within an aggressive black bear's territory.

programme.title: Blood Glacier
programme.desc: Scientists discover that a red liquid leaking from a nearby glacier contains organisms that turn living hosts into deadly mutants.

programme.title: Cabin Fever: Patient Zero
programme.desc: Vacationers think they've found the perfect place for a bachelor party, but a deadly, flesh-eating virus is waiting to infect them.

programme.title: Centigrade
programme.desc: A man and his pregnant wife become trapped in their car when a storm buries them underneath layers of snow and ice. As supplies dwindle and temperatures plunge, the couple must battle the elements and hypothermia in a desperate fight for survival.

programme.title: Come True
programme.desc: Strange events plague a troubled teen when she participates in a scientific experiment to stop her nightmares.

programme.title: Dark Was the Night
programme.desc: When a logging company decimates a forest in a small town, a sheriff (Kevin Durand) and his deputy (Lukas Haas) must confront the violent and unexplainable events that follow.

programme.title: Feral
programme.desc: A wild animal attacks six grad students on a weekend hike in the woods. One by one, they become infected with an unknown virus that turns them into bloodthirsty creatures.

programme.title: Dementia
programme.desc: Hired as a live-in nurse, a seemingly sweet woman (Kristina Klebe) unleashes her dark side as she torments a Vietnam War veteran (Gene Jones) who has dementia.

programme.title: Hellbender
programme.desc: A teen and her mother live simply in a home in the woods, spending their time making metal music. A chance encounter with a fellow teen causes her to uncover a connection between her family and witchcraft, which causes a rift with her mother.

programme.title: The Houses October Built 2
programme.desc: Still recovering from their abduction last Halloween, friends head back out on the road in search of the ultimate spooky experience.

programme.title: Impetigore
programme.desc: A woman inherits a house in her ancestral village, but she is unaware that members of the community have been trying to locate and kill her to remove the curse that has plagued them for years.

programme.title: Narcopolis
programme.desc: In the near future, Frank, a police officer, discovers that the legalization of all recreational drugs comes with a price.

programme.title: No Man's Land
programme.desc: When a vigilante border patrol turns fatal, a man flees on horseback to Mexico, seeking forgiveness from the victim's father.

programme.title: The Postcard Killings
programme.desc: A New York detective investigates the death of his daughter, who was murdered while on her honeymoon in London, the victim of an apparent serial killer.

programme.title: Puppet Master
programme.desc: Psychics (Paul Le Mat, Irene Miracle, Matt Roe) at a bay hotel meet the puppets Pin Head, Leech Woman, Tunneler, Blade and Jester.

programme.title: Spare Parts
programme.desc: Weapons attached to one arm, four young women must fight like gladiators as a bloodthirsty crowd cheers them on.

programme.title: State of Emergency
programme.desc: Survivors band together after an explosion at a military bioweapons plant turns most of their neighbors into flesh-eating zombies.

programme.title: Stranded
programme.desc: Astronauts (Christian Slater, Brendan Fehr) come under attack by dangerous alien spores after a meteor collides with their moon base.

programme.title: All Hallows' Eve 2
programme.desc: A woman finds a VHS tape that shows a series of gruesome tales.

programme.title: Almost Human
programme.desc: Seth sees his best friend disappear in a flash of light. Two years later, Seth is still dealing with the fallout and suspicions when a series of grisly murders rocks the community.

programme.title: Raanjhanaa
programme.desc: A small-town boy must bridge the class gap to win back childhood sweetheart.

programme.title: Dhamaal
programme.desc: Four lazy friends-Roy, Manav, Aditya, and Boman-race to find hidden treasure in Goa. Their adventure turns risky when Inspector Kabir discovers their plan and begins pursuing them.

programme.title: Rathnam
programme.desc: Rathnam, a henchman for MLA Panner Selvam, saves a girl from rowdies in Vellore. As her guardian angel, he must navigate danger to keep her safe.

programme.title: Bajrangi Bhaijaan
programme.desc: Pawan, a devotee of Lord Hanuman, finds a speech-impaired girl in Haryana. Learning she's from Pakistan, he risks everything to reunite her with her family.

programme.title: Uri: The Surgical Strike
programme.desc: Major Vihaan Shergill leads a covert operation against militants in Uri, avenging the soldiers killed in an attack and restoring security to the region.

programme.title: Jail (2009)
programme.desc: Parag's life takes a turn when he is framed for possessing drugs and ends up in jail.

programme.title: Raazi
programme.desc: Sehmat Khan, an undercover RAW agent, is married into a Pakistani family by her father so that she can get some valuable piece of information about the enemy.

programme.title: Satyamev Jayate
programme.desc: Framed cop battles the corrupt system to clear his name.

programme.title: Spyder
programme.desc: An intelligence officer battles a deranged serial killer who murders just to hear loved ones cry, racing to save the city before more lives are heartbreakingly lost.

programme.title: Holiday
programme.desc: Muskaan falls for Dino while holidaying, but father objects, suspecting him.

programme.title: Mission Raniganj
programme.desc: Jaswant Singh Gill, a mining engineer, bravely leads a rescue mission to save 65 miners stuck inside a flooded coal mine, risking everything for their lives and safety.

programme.title: Wanted
programme.desc: Bad boy gangster falls for good girl, but can he change his ways for love?

programme.title: Baaghi
programme.desc: Ronny, expelled for mischief, enters a Kerala martial arts academy and forms a tense bond with Sia. Rivalry with powerful Raghav tests love, loyalty, and survival.

programme.title: Naya Khoon
programme.desc: Siblings Vikram and Seema have contrasting personalities. Vikram is an alcoholic while Seema is a doctor. When Seema decides to marry a poor Anand, her brother opposes her decision.

programme.title: Silence.Can You Hear It?
programme.desc: When everyone is hiding the truth, justice will thrive despite the silence. Prepare for a murder mystery that will keep you guessing till the end.

programme.title: Bachchhan Paandey
programme.desc: When a struggling filmmaker and actor set out to make a biography on one of the most dreaded gangsters, they have little idea about just how crazy and dangerous it's going to be.

programme.title: Coolie No. 1 (1995)
programme.desc: A matchmaker schemes to humble arrogant Hoshiyar Chand. Chaos erupts when Raju, a coolie, pretends to be a millionaire to woo Chand's daughter, sparking hilarity and unexpected twists.

programme.title: Laadla
programme.desc: Sheetal, a stubborn factory owner, marries union leader Raju to tame him, but ends up learning surprising lessons about power, love, and pride.

programme.title: The Hero: Love Story of a Spy
programme.desc: Arun is an Indian spy who falls in love with Reshma. However, he gets shattered after Reshma goes missing during a mission and vows to punish the perpetrators.

programme.title: Dabangg
programme.desc: Chulbul Pandey faces off against Baccha Singh, a local goon and aspiring politician. The conflict escalates when Chulbul kills Baccha Singh's younger brother, intensifying their personal vendetta.

programme.title: Khoonkhar
programme.desc: Gagan and Sweety fall in love. Her father disapproves their relationship. While defending a family from thugs one day, Gagan unknowingly saves her.

programme.title: Fitoor
programme.desc: A young Kashmiri boy Noor tries to win the heart of his muse Firdaus , while her mother hatches a scheme to end his quest for true love.

programme.title: Zid
programme.desc: Maya falls in love with Ronnie, a crime reporter, and gets obsessed with him. Things take a turn for the worse when they both get involved in a murder case.

programme.title: Acharya
programme.desc: A stranger arrives in a corrupt village to fulfill a comrade's wish by ending illegal practices and challenging powerful leaders.

programme.title: Hogi Pyaar Ki Jeet
programme.desc: Two brothers reunite in order to fulfil the wish of their dying father whom Thakur bitterly humiliates and murders. They intend to marry Thakur's two daughters to seek revenge.

programme.title: Turtle
programme.desc: Ramkaran churns the dry earth of a village in search of water to quench his thirst.

programme.title: Operation Romeo
programme.desc: Chaos ensues when a young man and a woman in love meet two strangers.

programme.title: Madaari
programme.desc: Nirmal kidnaps the son of the home minister and forces the government to meet his demands.

programme.title: Daddy
programme.desc: Arun Gawli becomes one of India's most notable gangsters before he ventures into politics.

programme.title: Go Goa Gone
programme.desc: Trouble strikes when three friends get stranded on an island infested with zombies.

programme.title: Mom
programme.desc: Devki vows to get revenge after her stepdaughter's rapists are acquitted by the court.

programme.title: Tejas
programme.desc: Tejas, a pilot, faces the trauma of her past while she is on a mission to rescue an Indian spy.

programme.title: Mrs. Chatterjee vs Norway
programme.desc: An Indian woman fights for justice after Norwegian authorities take her children away.

programme.title: Laila Majnu
programme.desc: Laila and Qais's love is tested by societal pressures and family expectations.

programme.title: Dhadak 2
programme.desc: Chaos erupts when Nilesh and Vidhi fall in love despite their caste differences.

programme.title: Mukkabaaz
programme.desc: Shravan, a boxer, finds himself at loggerheads with Bhagwan, who runs a boxing federation.

programme.title: Raman Raghav 2.0
programme.desc: A serial killer stalks a drug-addicted detective investigating the killer's past crimes.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which knowledge will only take you so far, as patience and lateral thinking are also vital. A team of insurance professionals face off against a trio of committed gamblers. Who will win the battle as they compete to draw together the connections between things, which, at first glance, seem utterly random - such as Prince of Darkness, Bambi, Grocer and Tarzan?

programme.title: The American Revolution
programme.desc: The Revolution, now a war for American independence, faces its toughest challenge yet as General Washington tries to defend New York City from invasion by sea. The resulting Battle of Long Island is a huge defeat for the Americans, who narrowly escape and spend the next several months on the run. In late December, Washington’s army regroups and prepares to attack an outpost in Trenton, New Jersey. A film by Ken Burns, Sarah Botstein and David Schmidt

programme.title: The American Revolution
programme.desc: As America braces for a third year of devastating war, British commanders launch campaigns to take Albany and Philadelphia. General Howe beats General Washington at Brandywine and Germantown, while the Continental Congress flees Philadelphia. But the Americans’ surprising victory over General Burgoyne at Saratoga opens the door for France to officially support the United States. A film by Ken Burns, Sarah Botstein and David Schmidt

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The quarter-finals continue. Two more teams return for another qualification match; both have won one quarter-final already, and a second win will guarantee a place in the semis. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which knowledge will only take you so far, as patience and lateral thinking are also vital. Three senior managers from a national bookstore face off against a team drawn together by their love of London's retro bowling alleys. Who will win the battle as they compete to draw together the connections between things, which, at first glance, seem utterly random?

programme.title: Universe
programme.desc: A space mission discovers the dramatic history of the Milky Way. Professor Brian Cox reveals how our galaxy endured multiple collisions as rival galaxies fought for survival.

programme.title: The Nazis: A Warning from History
programme.desc: Adolf Hitler loved to watch feature films, and he liked one film in particular, the Hollywood epic The Lives of a Bengal Lancer. 'It was certainly his favourite film,' says Herbert Dohring, a member of Hitler's SS bodyguard, 'and he would always talk about it - this huge English empire - how such a relatively small people could establish and manage something like that.' Hitler would later say, 'What India was for England, the territory of Russia will be for us.' How was it possible then, that in 1939 Hitler found himself at war with a country whose achievements he admired, Great Britain, and allied to his ideological enemy, the Soviet Union? With the help of archive footage and interviews with eyewitnesses, including former diplomats and members of the Nazi party who had never appeared on television before, this film charts the course of Hitler's road to war.

programme.title: One Foot in the Past
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark investigates how stately homes go about opening up their kitchens and servants quarters to visitors. We go behind the scenes at Erdigg, Ham House and Chatsworth.

programme.title: Remembers...
programme.desc: Writer Peter Flannery looks back on his experiences creating the acclaimed 1996 drama Our Friends in the North. Following the lives of four friends from Newcastle over a period of three decades, the series struck a chord with the nation’s viewers and turned its young cast into household names. In this introduction to the series, Peter shares the behind-the-scenes story of how the drama was made - an epic tale in its own right - and assesses the impact it had on audiences, the cast and those who helped create what is today considered a landmark piece of television.

programme.title: Our Friends in the North
programme.desc: First episode of a nine-part drama charting 30 years in the lives of four Geordie friends from 1964-1995. While Nicky decides he wants to change the world and Geordie heads for London, Mary and Tosker make a life-changing mistake.

programme.title: Our Friends in the North
programme.desc: 1966: While Harold Wilson and The Beatles rule England, everything has changed for the four from Newcastle.

programme.title: Our Friends in the North
programme.desc: 1967: Geordie flirts with danger in his affair with Barratt's mistress. Mary and Tosker's flat and marriage are both falling apart.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of quarter-finals concludes with an elimination match. Two teams return having lost their first quarter-finals; a second loss here means they leave the competition. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which knowledge will only take you so far, as patience and lateral thinking are also vital. In the first of the quarter-finals, three fans of the Archers radio series take on the combined knowledge of three flatmates who all met up at Exeter University. They compete to draw together the connections between things, which, at first glance, seem utterly random - from Castor and Pollux to Brahma to Yoshi to Orville the Duck.

programme.title: Double Cross: The True Story of the D-Day Spies
programme.desc: The story of D-Day has been told from the point of view of the soldiers who fought in it, the tacticians who planned it and the generals who led it. But that epic event in world history has never been told before through the perspective of the strange handful of spies who made it possible. D-Day was a great victory of arms, a tactical coup, and a moral crusade. But it was also a triumph for espionage, deceit, and thinking of the most twisted sort. Following on from his hugely successful BBC Two documentaries, Operation Mincemeat and Double Agent: The Eddie Chapman Story (Agent Zigzag), writer and presenter Ben Macintyre returns to the small screen to bring to life his third best-selling book - Double Cross The True Story of the D-Day Spies. Macintyre reveals the gripping true story of five of the double agents who helped to make D-Day such a success.

programme.title: This World
programme.desc: Simon Reeve heads to Vietnam to uncover the stories behind the morning pick-me-up. He travels to the central highlands, where he meets the people who grow, pick and pack coffee.

programme.title: The Producers
programme.desc: A struggling producer and timid accountant scheme to make money by putting on a Broadway show they know will flop, so none of the numerous investors will expect their cash back. A tasteless glorification of Hitler and the Nazis looks guaranteed to fail.

programme.title: Mel Brooks and Me - by Alan Yentob
programme.desc: Alan Yentob looks back on his experiences working with the great Mel Brooks, who was awarded an Honorary Oscar in 2024 in recognition of a boundary-breaking career that shocked audiences but always left them laughing too. Alan reflects on what it was like being a producer encountering the comic genius who created The Producers and how his and Mel’s working relationship quickly developed into something that one newspaper critic once described as nothing less than a love story.

programme.title: imagine...
programme.desc: At the age of 91, Mel Brooks is unstoppable, with his musical Young Frankenstein opening to great critical acclaim in London in late 2017. Alan Yentob visits Mel at home in Hollywood, at work and at play. With the aid of BBC archive stretching back decades, together they embark on an unpredictable, irresistible journey through the city of stars, meeting the legendary Carl Reiner along the way. The driver is Mel Brooks - you have been warned!

programme.title: Acoustic at the BBC
programme.desc: A journey through some of the finest moments of acoustic guitar performances from the BBC archives - from Jimmy Page's television debut in 1958 to Oasis and Biffy Clyro. programme.title: The Media Show
programme.desc: Following the sentencing of 13 people for violent disorder during protests in Southampton, attention has turned to the role of content creators who film demonstrations and public disorder. The judge cited the usefulness of footage filmed by so-called 'citizen journalists'. Siddharth Venkataramakrishnan of the Institute for Strategic Dialogue and content creator Wesley Winter give their perspective on the issue. The BBC has begun setting out changes as part of plans to deliver significant savings across the organisation. The measures include the closure of several programmes across radio and current affairs output, alongside wider reductions in staffing. Jonathan Munro, global director of BBC News, outlines the changes. Norma Percy has spent years getting to the heart of some of the biggest moments in recent history. Her documentaries have been celebrated for talking directly to the people who made the decisions. As the BBC airs her latest film - about Brexit - she reflects on her storied career and the future of documentary-making.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: A trusted GP manager fiddles the books to pocket over £300,000 to fund her lavish spending. And the man who used the Covid pandemic to steal over £100,000 from the public purse.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin has a friendly disagreement with a developer about what to do at a Northampton flat. Jacqui is in Norwich, where there’s potential to convert a former shop into a flat. Dion is in Stoke, where new windows are part of a long to-do list.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross discovers how nature influenced one Victorian printer to design banknotes for countries around the world. Thomas Forrester shows Charlie a collection of rare and valuable examples, including detailed early designs and specimen notes. It’s all about the money too for the red and blue teams as they battle it out for bargains at an antiques centre near Stratford-upon-Avon. Best friends Sandra and Florence and their expert Tim Weeks go head-to-head with father and son Jason and Andy and expert Izzie Balmer. Will Andy’s love of toy cars outmanoeuvre Sandra’s passion for teapots? Over in Lichfield, what will auctioneer Richard Winterton make of the teams’ lots? And which team will make the most profit and win the day?

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Policing Paradise
programme.desc: It's carnival time, one of the biggest celebrations of the summer season in Hamilton. Community Officer PC Chris Douglas is preparing for the island’s annual parade, swapping uniform for a handmade, colourful costume, while his colleagues stay on duty to keep the celebrations running smoothly as thousands line the streets to dance and party. Acting Inspector Tiffany Caisey and Sergeant Mervin Homer move from roadside stops to an unusual call at Dockyard, where a missing phone leads them to a cruise ship packed with 3,000 passengers and the tricky task of locating it on board. In central Hamilton, Sergeant Helen McHugh, K9 handler PC Robin MacNab and her dog Roni head into a park linked to drug activity tracking down a man, who when searched, reveals he’s in possession of a crack pipe and associated drug paraphernalia. Coastguard Corporal Quentin Burgess and Private Matthew Stephens are patrolling the waters, carrying out checks ahead of a busy maritime weekend. In the the run-up to carnival, Officer Chris teams up with PC Krishna Singh and PC Oba Swan to offer crime prevention advice to locals, a reminder of the close community ties that shape island life.

programme.title: Scam Interceptors
programme.desc: Cruel scammers force a woman into missing a vital medical appointment under the pretence her internet is being hacked. But it's all lies that have kept the victim tied up with them for over an hour. The ground interceptors battle heavy traffic to try to warn her before the scammer clears out her bank account. Later, the team confront the criminals behind the scam in a showdown that needs to be seen to be believed.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Richie Anderson is in rural Lincolnshire, helping John and Margaret find a large countryside home, with enough room for six visiting grandchildren, on a budget of £400,000. They explore wonderful properties, including one with a delightful garden, as well as a mystery house close to the coast. Richie also visits a former 300-acre farm now being transformed through an ambitious rewilding project.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chris Hull and Flo Headlam visit Didcot to transform a garden that has been abandoned to the elements into a Bridgerton-inspired garden with a safe play area for a family's three energetic boys.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Joyce and her husband Mike spent more than 40 years living together in Guernsey. But after they both faced health challenges, they decided to return to Mike’s beloved home county of Yorkshire for a fresh start. Just months after settling in, tragedy struck when Mike passed away unexpectedly, leaving Joyce heartbroken and surrounded by a lifetime of their shared treasures. Now, with the support of close friends Brian and Adele, Joyce is ready to let go of some of the old items that have filled her new home. She hopes the auction will help her gain some space, while raising money to support her son’s work rescuing stray dogs in Thailand, honouring the couple’s shared love of animals. Auctioneer James Broad and restorer Saf Fakir roll up in West Yorkshire to lend a hand. JB uncovers pocket watches, a Victorian cauldron and a trove of jewellery, while Saf takes on three challenging restorations, including an oak coal scuttle, a writing slope and a rare pair of brass coffin stands. With a big mix of lots heading under the hammer, can JB and Saf help Joyce turn Mike’s collections into a lasting legacy and raise enough to help a cause they cared so deeply about?

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The brainy quiz returns - but this time host Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Lucy Porter. As always, contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.desc: Alex Jones and Vernon Kay bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The pressure mounts for Eve and Suki on the day of their adoption hearing. Phil gives Billy some words of advice.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The Bear Ladies fix an iconic mascot from the 1966 World Cup, Suzie repairs a well-travelled leather hat, and Pete and Sonnaz bring the music back to a battered old washboard.

programme.title: Nursery Scandals: What Went Wrong?
programme.desc: Following the death of a toddler at a ‘good’ rated nursery, Hayley Hassall investigates problems in early years care. Does the system designed to protect young children respond quickly enough when problems arise – and what happens when it fails? Focusing on three cases which had devastating consequences, Hayley hears from the parents of young children let down by safeguarding errors - and the whistleblowers who tried to prevent them. Contains upsetting scenes.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage as Scotland play their final Group C match against Brazil in Miami. The match is the fifth World Cup meeting between the two sides and a repeat of the opening fixture of the 1998 tournament, where Scotland were unfortunate to be beaten 2-1 in a close-fought encounter in Paris. Five-time winners Brazil will hunt for a sixth title under the guidance of five-time Champions League-winning manager Carlo Ancelotti, who took over in May last year. Despite a host of attacking talent, the Selecao endured a turbulent qualifying campaign, finishing fifth in the South American group, and haven’t lifted the trophy since 2002.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage of the final game in Group A, where Czech Republic take on hosts Mexico in Mexico City. Czech Republic last qualified for the World Cup finals 20 years ago, when they exited at the group stage. Mexico were also eliminated at the first hurdle four years ago in Qatar and will be hoping that home support can improve their fortunes this time around. Czech Republic won the only previous match between the two countries, a friendly in Hong Kong in 2000.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: A criminal gang sets up ‘benefits factories’ to defraud UK taxpayers out of a whopping £50 million pounds. And a Swansea shop owner selling illegal vapes dodges £100,000 in tax.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Programme in which experts uncover the various tricks of the property auction trade

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: As the red and blue teams weave their way around an antiques arcade in Hungerford, Caroline Hawley tries her hand at weaving willow. In the workshop of Erica Adams, she learns about the history of wicker and its resurgence as a sustainable material for making baskets and furniture. On the red team, couple Jonjo and Scarlett are aiming to 'write themselves into Bargain Hunt history', with some expert help from Raj Bisram. The art deco fans soon spot a stylish period coffee set, but will they decide to splash out? Over with the blue team, Stephanie Connell is the guide for husband-and-wife duo Liz and Ian. Liz wants to buy something 'quirky, different and unique', while Ian wants to go 'big and bold'. Animal-related antiques seem to be the order or the day - but will an owl money box or a ceramic cobra win them over? Down the road in Newbury, auctioneer Thomas Forrester puts the teams’ items under the gavel, but which team will come out on top?

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Policing Paradise
programme.desc: A stop-and-search uncovers cannabis, CCTV supports a mall assault inquiry, and officers track risky teen riders as a long-serving officer ends her shift with major career news.

programme.title: Scam Interceptors
programme.desc: A scammer posing as a broadband internet provider tries to steal money from a man by remotely accessing his device – but the team in the scam hub have other ideas. Repeated efforts to get through to the target by Rav and Nick fail, leading to a race against time as ground interceptor Nathan hits the road to try and prevent the scam in person. With serious cash on the line and a highly persistent scammer, there are no guarantees, and Rav finds himself in a tit-for-tat with the man unsure who to believe.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sun, sea and seals... it’s another glorious escape to the country in Cornwall, where Alistair Appleton is helping a retired couple from the Wirral find their perfect Cornish retreat within walking distance of a pub. After years of happy family holidays there, and now with their son and new grandson living locally, they’re ready to make the move and live in Cornwall full-time. With a £400,000 budget and a love of bungalows, the couple are given some tempting options by Alistair. He also pops by the Cornish Seal Sanctuary to meet some of its permanent and temporary residents.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Lee Burkhill and Chris Hull are in Tamworth to transform mum Jade and daughter Charlie’s tiny, unloved plot into a pink-inspired garden. They both love all shades of the colour, from magenta to mauve, and feel this would be a perfect palette for their forever home. Can the team achieve all this on a £4,500 budget?

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Izzie and JJ help Michael and Clara clear their busy home and revive forgotten gems to sell at auction, but will they raise enough to give Michael’s parents a day out to remember?

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The brainy quiz returns - but this time host Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Lucy Porter. As always, contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Nicola and family celebrate half Christmas. Suki and Eve’s moment of happiness is short-lived

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon is back in the host’s chair for the award-winning comedy panel show, with Lee Mack and David Mitchell returning as team captains. Each week, a stellar cast of celebrity guests reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up? Sorting fact from fiction this week are guests Raj Bisram, Deborah Frances-White, Stephen Mulhern and Jenny Ryan.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage as Ecuador play Germany in their final Group E match at the New Jersey Stadium in New York. Four-time winners Germany have uncharacteristically struggled at the past two World Cups, going out in the group stage on both occasions. Ecuador proved incredibly hard to beat in South American qualifying, conceding just five times in 18 matches as they finished second behind Argentina and ahead of the likes of Brazil and Colombia. These two teams have met once before at a World Cup, with Germany winning 3-0 in the group stage of the 2006 tournament.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage of the final match in Group F in Dallas between two former World Cup hosts, Japan and Sweden. Sweden reached the quarter-finals in their last World Cup appearance in 2018, where they were beaten by England. Japan are at their seventh consecutive finals but have never previously made it past the first knockout round.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: The Environment Agency gets called in when a waste site flouts the rules and local residents start complaining. The facility is supposed to be a plastics recycling plant, but lorry loads of illegal waste are being delivered on a regular basis. Investigators decide to take a look, and what they find rings alarm bells – piles and piles of waste covering the site. Not only is it way beyond what’s permitted to be on a plastics recycling site, but more worryingly, it’s not being stored properly and there’s a genuine risk of it catching fire as a result. The Environment Agency takes swift action. In a second case, Trading Standards are called in after a spate of fake Sky TV remote controls flood the local market. As officers start to investigate, they unearth a massive operation in fake goods being sold on online auction sites. As well as remote controls, the seller has electrical items and cosmetics up for sale, all pretending to be from well-known brands. It’s putting consumers at risk and running up a massive bill in unpaid tax – money which should be in the public purse.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 22-year-old developer tackles a very ambitious project in Merseyside, Martel is optimistic about the prospects of a house in County Durham, and Martin falls for the charms of a characterful bungalow in Dorset.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: They have been raising smiles for centuries, and in this episode, Eric Knowles learns about a unique clowning tradition. No two clowns should look alike, which is why their faces are copyrighted – on painted eggs! Professional clown Mattie Faint prompts a chuckle or two from Eric when he explains this egg-centric custom. Topping the bill on this show are two teams of antiques hunters, browsing hundreds of stalls at a huge fair with experts Nick Hall and Izzie Balmer. With 60 minutes on the clock, grandmother and granddaughter Karen and Amelia stew over an art deco tea set, while the reds, friends Stuart and Martin, reckon they spy a profit with a pair of mother of pearl opera glasses. Selling their items is auctioneer Charles Hanson in nearby Derbyshire. Which team is his money on?

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Under the Vines
programme.desc: After the trip, Tippy disappears, Louis considers making a grand gesture, and Gus faces a big choice.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple to come back to Blighty after 16 years down under. Their £450,000 budget might not stretch quite as far in Lancashire as it did in Oz, but Jules still has a great selection of properties that are reminiscent of the home they have left behind. He also manages to squeeze in a visit to a nature reserve, created at a quarry used to build the UK’s first motorway.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock returns to Hampshire to see one of the gardens she designed with the Rich Brothers. Sheena and Jim had wanted a relaxed, seaside-inspired space for entertaining, and Charlie is delighted to see how they have truly made it their own. Charlie also heads back to Coventry to revisit the fun-filled activity garden she created for Louise and her children, now transformed into a mature haven brimming with shrubs and flowers. Finally, Charlie catches up with Debs and Nige in Oldham, whose dream of a mid-century–inspired garden has blossomed into a stylish retreat perfect for family and friends.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Bee and Lauren hunt for treasure at a retired butcher’s home in Leeds. Sniffing out a collection of Troika pottery, they help twice-widowed Michael relive a special family holiday.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The brainy quiz returns - but this time host Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Lucy Porter. As always, contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and JB Gill bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: What Happened at...?
programme.desc: On 1 July 1916, nearly 20,000 British soldiers were killed on the first day of the Battle of the Somme – one of the bloodiest days in the history of warfare and a lasting symbol of both sacrifice and futility. In this documentary, Jordan Dunbar examines how communities have been shaped by the battle and reveals how its legacy continues to resonate through families, including his own.

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a special programme in the wake of Sir Keir Starmer announcing his resignation. With Andy Burnham now back at Westminster and the favourite to become the next prime minister, a panel of politicians and commentators field questions from our audience in Kettering about what all of this could mean for the country.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The arrival of a long-lost brother at a family-run golf club coincides with the discovery of a body on the course. But a prime suspect with an alibi leaves the team with an impenetrable conundrum.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Madonna & Graham
programme.desc: Graham Norton meets with Madonna for an in-depth discussion about her incredible life and career.

programme.title: St Denis Medical
programme.desc: Joyce hosts a ribbon-cutting ceremony for the birthing centre, Matt and Serena take on an awkward assignment, and Ron tries to knock out a quick physical before his fishing trip.

programme.title: St Denis Medical
programme.desc: When a doctor becomes a patient, his co-workers rally around him, whether he likes it or not. Meanwhile, Matt and Serena need to talk.

programme.title: King Richard
programme.desc: Armed with a clear vision and a brazen 78-page plan, Richard Williams is determined to write his daughters, Venus and Serena, into history. Training rain or shine on Compton, California's neglected tennis courts, the girls are shaped by their father's unyielding commitment and their mother's balanced perspective and keen intuition, defying the seemingly insurmountable odds and expectations laid before them. Based on a true story, the film follows the journey of a family whose unwavering resolve and unconditional belief ultimately deliver two of the world's greatest sports legends.

programme.title: Mood
programme.desc: Sasha thinks she’s found a best friend in Carly and her life is on the up, partying and having fun, but she still isn’t making enough money online. Carly says Sasha needs to drop the attitude and learn the importance of building a brand. An ecstatic Sasha is booked for an event in an exclusive five-star hotel. Performing a song for the guests, Sasha is the toast of the evening until Carly manipulates Sasha into making a decision she never thought she would. Sasha is left questioning whether this glittering world of status and money is all it seems.

programme.title: Roman Kemp: The Fight for Young Lives
programme.desc: On a daily basis, Roman Kemp is contacted by young people who are struggling. With people turning to him for advice, he’s questioning whether or not there is a worsening mental health crisis and asking if these young people should be offered more support in their own communities. This documentary follows Roman as he immerses himself in schools, youth centres and treatment settings around the country, meets with experts and charities, and visits the Houses of Parliament to try to understand the potential solutions for improving the mental health of young people in the UK today.

programme.title: Walking With Dinosaurs
programme.desc: Based on an unprecedented discovery in Alberta, Canada, this episode tells the epic tale of a Pachyrhinosaurus herd, one of the largest dinosaur groups that ever lived, through the eyes of its young member Albie.

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Wildlife documentary telling the story of Moja, a five-month-old lion cub living with his single lioness mum, Nyota, in Kenya's Masai Mara. Together, they face a fight for life.

programme.title: Walking With Dinosaurs
programme.desc: The tale of one of the largest dinosaurs ever to walk the earth, a colossal, long-necked Lusotitan. Living 150 million years ago on an island known as the Iberian Mesta, now in Portugal, this 25-metre-long titan dwarfs every other dinosaur in the region. Yet its intimidating size is only part of the story. The ultimate goal of the male of the species is to win over a female and secure his legacy. It’s a quest that sees him embark on an epic journey as he risks his life for love.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May buy old British roadsters from Jensen-Healey, Lotus and TVR before taking a wistful journey through the remnants of the UK's sports car industry, starting in Norfolk and ending up at the mothballed TVR factory in Blackpool. Also, Jeremy takes to the test track in the sensational new Ferrari 458 Italia, and laid-back Hollywood legend Jeff Goldblum is the Star in the new Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: I Kissed a Girl
programme.desc: Emotions are still running high after last night's dinner party, and with feelings heating up, Dannii arrives with a chemistry test that really puts the girls' heads in a spin. Teamed with a surprise announcement that the first Kiss-Off is only hours away, it’s crunch time in the masseria for our couples. As two girls go head to head, vying for the same girl's affection, one drops the ‘L’ word and another sheds a tear. But whose summer of love is about to come to an abrupt end?

programme.title: I Kissed a Girl
programme.desc: After an intense Kiss-Off, the masseria waves goodbye to its first girl. But when cracks start to emerge in couples, candid conversations leave some feeling unsettled. An invitation from Cupid to attend a chemistry test under the stars leads the girls to a seductive evening full of temptation. Dannii oversees a spicy night, culminating with one heart-racing surprise.

programme.title: Road House
programme.desc: Ex-UFC fighter Dalton is persuaded to take a job as a bouncer at a Florida Keys road house, only to learn that the bar is being terrorised by a gang working for a local crime boss.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: An investigation into a viral prison sex tape leads journalist Mobeen Azhar to discover an illicit prison economy fuelled by inappropriate relationships, coercion and contraband.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: Mobeen’s investigation into prison corruption hears of the murder of a former officer who had been involved in uncovering an illicit relationship between a guard and an inmate.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May buy old British roadsters from Jensen-Healey, Lotus and TVR before taking a wistful journey through the remnants of the UK's sports car industry, starting in Norfolk and ending up at the mothballed TVR factory in Blackpool. Also, Jeremy takes to the test track in the sensational new Ferrari 458 Italia, and laid-back Hollywood legend Jeff Goldblum is the Star in the new Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Calamity James is a slapstick comedy about a young man, James, who is cursed with ‘acute misfortune syndrome’. James tries to reconnect with his dad after being thrown out by his mum for setting fire to their house at a family barbecue. In this world, chronic bad luck exists and the unbelievable is the everyday.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Jack is an unfulfilled actor who is desperate for some meaningful work. He agrees to join fellow thespian Lauren on a pantomime tour of the north east. Just one catch: it’s an off-season tour of dementia homes, where the audience tend to be more interested in daytime TV than in Jack and Lauren’s contemporary interpretation of Alice in Wonderland. Lauren remains relentlessly upbeat, but Jack fears he’s sunk to a new low... until he is unexpectedly confronted with the reality of life, his future and where it ends.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Wildlife presenter Steve Backshall is on a quest to meet 60 of the deadliest animals on the planet. Steve and his team are on a fun-filled, adrenalin-fuelled journey to find those animals most people spend their lifetime avoiding. The action kicks off in Hoedspruit, South Africa. The adventure is nearly over before it's even begun as an angry hippo surprises Steve in a tense encounter on the river. Unscathed and undaunted by the first deadly animal on his list, Steve gets up close and personal with some of South Africa's deadliest snakes and spends the night under the stars in order to find a particularly venomous scorpion.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Wildlife presenter Steve Backshall is on a quest to meet 60 of the deadliest animals on the planet. Steve and his team are on a fun-filled, adrenalin-fuelled journey to find those animals most people spend their lifetime avoiding. When a Nile crocodile attacks a local girl in her own swimming pool, Steve decides to investigate these ancient reptiles and to judge for himself whether they deserve their deadly reputation. He pits himself against the mighty fish eagle as he tries his hand at diving for his supper, and takes to the South African skies to try and find one of Africa's most elusive predators, the awesome hunting dog.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May compare the Mercedes SLS, the Porsche 911 GT3 RS and the Ferrari 458 Italia in an epic road trip up America's east coast. Along the way they undertake a series of challenges culminating in a fight to find the fastest way into New York, all in the name of crowning the world's greatest sports car. Plus a round-up of car-related Christmas stuff and another celebrity takes to the track as the star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: The Traitors US
programme.desc: The latest mission offers the chance to stop the night's murder – but only if the Faithful stick together. After an explosive Round Table, one Traitor is under pressure from an influential player. But can they murder him to stop him talking?

programme.title: The Traitors US
programme.desc: The Faithful plan a coup, and one Traitor is accused to their face. The Round Table brings difficult decisions, and with the temperature in the turret at boiling point, the Traitors must pull off the bloodiest murder this castle has ever seen.

programme.title: OnlyFans: Inside the Machine
programme.desc: Behind the billion-pound success of OnlyFans lies a hidden ecosystem of exploitation. A BBC Current Affairs investigation uncovers allegations of exploitation, coercion and violence committed by some of the hundreds of agents who manage many of the platform’s creators as well as the potential for modern slavery. Through survivor testimony, undercover access to a managers’ chat forum and expert legal analysis, the film exposes an industry where young women are trapped, threatened and profited from - raising urgent questions about accountability at the heart of one of Britain's most lucrative tech companies.

programme.title: Back to Life
programme.desc: When Miri Matteson returns home after eighteen years, can she integrate back into her old life? With a terrible event from her past hanging over her, it won’t be easy.

programme.title: Back to Life
programme.desc: When Miri Matteson returns home after eighteen years, can she integrate back into her old life? With a terrible event from her past hanging over her, it won’t be easy.

programme.desc: The Fire Station in Sunderland plays host to this heat of the BBC New Comedy Awards, where five hopefuls battle it out for a place in the grand final.

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards
programme.desc: Five hopefuls battle it out at the legendary Leith Theatre. Award-winning comedian Larry Dean is host, with judges Richard Gadd, Susie McCabe and head judge Josh Pugh.

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards
programme.desc: The contest heads to The Braid in Ballymena, County Antrim. Award-winning stand-up Shane Todd hosts with Diona Doherty, Martin Angolo and head judge Josh Pugh.

programme.title: Walking With Dinosaurs
programme.desc: The tale of one of the largest dinosaurs ever to walk the earth, a colossal, long-necked Lusotitan. Living 150 million years ago on an island known as the Iberian Mesta, now in Portugal, this 25-metre-long titan dwarfs every other dinosaur in the region.
